CIA Reportedly Prepares Criminal Referral of Tucker Carlson, as Israel and its US Loyalists Demand His Arrest

greenwald.substack.com/p/cia…

That Israel's most subversive foreign agents are trying to imprison Americans journalists for the crime of criticizing their sacred country is sickening enough.

That they're doing this by accusing OTHERS of being covert foreign agents is a level of chutzpah previously unknown.


Laura Loomer (@LauraLoomer)

I can’t wait to see Tucker Carlson go to prison.



— https://nitter.net/LauraLoomer/status/2033013980609003694#m

Totally agree with this, as long as "everyone" means "everyone."


AG (@AGHamilton29)

One of the best things the DoJ and FBI can do for the public discourse is to expose everyone taking money and directions from foreign governments and the intermediaries facilitating it. 

Expose it all, then the public can judge the reality of foreign influence.



— https://nitter.net/AGHamilton29/status/2033196837142687804#m

One of the heroes of the hard-core pro-Israel crowd in the US is a Harvard Professor of Yiddish named Ruth Wisse. She's one of those old-school neocons: wanting the US to serve Israel and destroy all its enemies.

Here is what she said is the duty of every Jewish American:

Video

Who the fuck are Israelis to dictate that American journalists should be arrested and prosecuted for "treason"?

As always, these Israelis are not alone. They have a horde of loyalists in the US echoing this.

By "treason," they mean: speaking and reporting critically on Israel.


פלר חסן נחום Fleur Hassan-Nahoum (@FleurHassanN)

Tucker Carlson should be arrested and tried for treason.



— https://nitter.net/FleurHassanN/status/2033096478269346058#m

nitter.net/TuckerCarlson/status/1…


Tucker Carlson (@TuckerCarlson)

Former Pentagon official Dan Caldwell explains what would happen if Ted Cruz gets his way in Iran.
 
(0:00) Introduction
(0:47) What Would Happen if the US Strikes Iran?
(9:23)

During the 12-day Israel/US/Iran war last year, former Trump DoD official @dandcaldwell (and others) warned of the rapid depletion of anti-missile systems and how it  forced an end to that war.

Totally predictable the same problems would emerge now for a far more intense war:


Phil Stewart (@phildstewart)

(Reuters) - Israel informed the U.S. this week that it is running critically low on ballistic missile interceptors as the conflict with Iran continues, Semafor reported on Saturday, citing U

Separately: Brazilian prisoners (even those convicted of the worst crimes) are often allowed to serve their sentence at home if they have a serious disease that makes prison confinement inhumane.

Bolsonaro -- due to his near-fatal stabbing on the 2018 campaign trail -- has suffered increasingly worse and life-threatening intestinal conditions. The 70-year-old former President has been repeatedly rushed from prison to the ER for treatment.

Despite this, Brazil's judge Alexandre de Moraes has ig

LULA BANS STATE DEPT'S DARREN BEATTIE FROM BRAZIL 🇧🇷🇺🇸

Since Trump's 2025 inauguration, Darren Beattie has been in high-level State Dept positions. Last month, he was appointed to oversee US/Brazil relations.

As part of that diplomatic work, he was scheduled to visit Brazil this week. Before he arrived, Lula ordered his visa revoked.

Authorities gave conflicting reasons. They first claim was that he lied on on his visa application by omitting his intent to visit Bolsonaro in prison. But Lula

For the first weeks of the Iraq War, journalists who showed things were far worse than Bush/Cheney claimed were demonized as traitors, pro-Saddam, etc. -- even though they were right.

Patriotism doesn't mean lying for the state about wars. It means telling the truth about them:


ABC News Politics (@ABCPolitics)

Even as Iran has been "pummeled" by Israeli and American air strikes, with its military capabilities greatly diminished, Tehran continues to demonstrate resolve, both in adapting and e

The delusional alternative reality you get to create and live in when you're a Silicon Valley billionaire whose husband is a top Trump State Dept official, and you're both fanatically pro-Israel. 

Look at the fairy tale land which these people inhabit: @rabois edition.👇

The enraged reaction to this NYT article about the synagogue attack is just lunacy.

Even for common crimes, news outlets include the perpetrator's likely motives (he was distraught over being fired; his wife left him; he got radicalized online, etc.). Nobody rational thinks that's designed to justify the crime: just to explain it.

Yet when a terrorist act happens, some try to erect a taboo around any reporting on the political motives.
